topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink swam on the page, a grotesque stain across the meticulously organized essay. I stared at the 'D-'. My hand, gripping the pen, started to tremble, the movement jerky and uncontrolled. It felt like a physical violation, a sourness blooming in my chest that made breathing shallow. I reread the feedback, the professor's marginalia sharp and dismissive. Every word seemed coated in contempt, aimed directly at my effort. I slowly closed the notebook, the sound of the cover snapping shut echoing loudly in the silent room. I pushed my chair back, the scrape of metal against the floor sounding unnecessarily harsh. I needed air, desperately, needing to scrub the taste of failure from my mouth.

[story 2]

"A C-minus," the automated voice announced from the screen, devoid of inflection. I didn't look up from the transcript. My knuckles were white where I clutched the paper. A hot flush crept up my neck, making my skin prickle. It wasn't just the letter; it was the sheer emptiness of the mark, a hollow echo where I’d expected recognition. I picked up the paper again, tracing the low grade with a fingertip, feeling a profound wave of nausea roll through me. I crumpled the paper slowly, deliberately, the sound of the tearing paper a small, pathetic release of tension. I shoved it deep into my backpack, eager to escape the sight of it.

[story 3]

I sat at the edge of my desk, the fluorescent lights buzzing overhead seeming to amplify the silence. The grade—a dismal F minus—was starkly visible on the digital portal. A cold, heavy weight settled in my stomach, making my shoulders slump forward as if physically bowed by the disappointment. I picked up my phone, my fingers fumbling slightly before I put the call straight to end. The sound of my own voice on the other end was tight, strained. "I... I don't understand," I managed, the words coming out thin and reedy. I could feel the heat rising in my cheeks, a burning sensation that had nothing to do with the room temperature. I just wanted to disappear, to make the entire situation vanish.
